codeReview.summary: The server covers the basic history, routing, attach, and callback behaviors, but two blocking protocol issues remain: it does not replay a detached state to reconnecting wrappers, and its new approval-input payload is not consumable by the wrapper.

issues.description: On register the server only replays attachment state when `attachedUser` is truthy. If a wrapper was previously attached, disconnects, and the user detaches while the session is offline, the wrapper keeps its old `attached=true` flag across reconnects (`wrapper/src/ws_client.rs:62-103` reuses the same `AtomicBool`, and `wrapper/src/ws_client.rs:221-228` only updates it on `attach-status-changed`). Because this branch sends nothing for the detached case, the wrapper can reconnect still thinking it is attached and will keep forwarding/accepting traffic even though the server state is detached.

`POST /sessions/:id/input` claims to support approval replies by transforming `{type:"approval", approved:boolean}` into `{type:"input", approval:boolean}`, but the wrapper never consumes that wire format. `wrapper/src/ws_client.rs:269-277` only turns `content`, `turn/start`, or `turn/interrupt` into codex stdin writes, so approval responses are silently ignored. That breaks the relay contract needed by the mission's approval flow (`features.json:109`, `validation-contract.md:187`).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The reconnect contract for attachment state is not documented in AGENTS or architecture: the wrapper preserves its `attached` flag across reconnects and depends on the server to replay the current status, including `attached:false` after offline detaches.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: wrapper/src/ws_client.rs:62-103 keeps the same `attached` AtomicBool through reconnect loops, and wrapper/src/ws_client.rs:221-228 only mutates it when an `attach-status-changed` frame arrives; server/src/relay-server.ts:341-345 currently replays only the attached=true case.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The approval-response wire format between bot, server, and wrapper is underspecified. Mission/validation docs require approval replies to traverse the relay, but the wrapper only documents/implements `turn/start` and `turn/interrupt` as inbound commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json:109 and validation-contract.md:187 require approval replies via the relay; server/src/relay-server.ts:654-658 emits `{type:"input", approval:boolean}`; wrapper/src/ws_client.rs:269-277 only handles `content`, `turn/start`, and `turn/interrupt`.

summary: Fail. I reviewed the mission docs, handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 750f4e7. The feature implements the requested REST endpoints/history buffering, but I found two blocking relay-contract issues: detached state is not replayed to reconnecting wrappers, and approval inputs are encoded in a format the wrapper ignores.
